Explicitly required in task prompt and verifier.py logic
Aerodynamic analysis of the eCRM-001 model at M0.78, 35k ft confirms physical plausibility for the lower bound.
Aerodynamic analysis of the eCRM-001 model at M0.78, 35k ft confirms physical plausibility for the upper bound.
Parsed eCRM-001_wing_tail.vsp3 XML, finding 'Wing', 'FuselageUpdated', 'Tail', 'VerticalTail' which perfectly match these substrings.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real NASA eCRM-001 conceptual aircraft model. I verified that the model geometry file contains 'FuselageUpdated', 'Wing', 'Tail', and 'VerticalTail', which perfectly match the required substring list ('wing', 'fuse', 'tail', 'vert') checked by the verifier logic. An aerodynamic verification of the model configuration at Mach 0.78 and 35,000 ft confirms that the true CD0 for this class of vehicle (approx. 0.015 to 0.036) is strictly bounded by the verifier's min (0.008) and max (0.050) thresholds. Both output file paths explicitly match the task instructions and export scripts.
